purpose: Evaluate one predeclared liquidity-admission overlay for the existing 14-day cross-sectional momentum research shadow. Liquidity may restrict new entries but may not force exits or create a new directional Alpha claim.

signal.source: strictly closed 15-minute OKX bars resampled to complete 4-hour bars
signal.known_at: 04:00 UTC after the signal bar closes
signal.entry: 04:00 UTC open; 15-minute delayed execution is a frozen falsification only

liquidity_overlay.entry_rule: new long and short members must be inside the top liquidity-eligible set and are then selected only by the frozen momentum ranking
liquidity_overlay.retention_rule: incumbents are never expelled by liquidity; they remain governed solely by the parent top-4 entry top-6 exit momentum hysteresis
liquidity_overlay.reason_for_fixed_12: the exact top-12 and 30-day definition was frozen in the earlier return-blind duplicate audit; this run evaluates it only as an execution overlay

baseline.name: 14-day momentum top-4 entry top-6 exit hysteresis without liquidity admission

falsification.randomization: circularly shift the complete target-weight path against the original tradable daily entry times

portfolio_increment_gates.comparison: 50 percent existing hysteresis plus 50 percent liquidity-admission overlay at unchanged unit gross exposure

prohibitions: Do not change the 30-day liquidity window, top-12 primary eligibility count, entry phase, momentum lookback, top-4 entry, top-6 exit, universe, direction, costs, or holding period after outcomes are opened.
Do not use liquidity to force incumbent exits after this protocol is locked.
Do not select the best result from the 10, 12, and 14 eligibility neighborhood.
Do not remove symbols, add price or volatility filters, or lower turnover and cost gates to rescue a failure.
Do not claim an independent Alpha family or assign a new H number.
Do not promote to formal A-tier from historical results.
Do not enable automatic ordering or position management.
